flashcards.back: An optimization technique that delays physical copying of memory until a write operation occurs.

flashcards.front: Key advantage of COW in fork()
flashcards.back: Avoids unnecessary copying when child process immediately calls exec(), saving memory and time.

flashcards.front: When does COW trigger a copy?
flashcards.back: When a process attempts to modify a shared memory page, causing a page fault.

flashcards.front: Use case of COW besides fork()
flashcards.back: Memory-mapped files and virtual memory management for efficient resource utilization.
Shared pages remain read-only and are not duplicated, allowing multiple processes to access the same physical page.
